messages.content:
In P2 is a formula =LIST!O2, which dispalys the value  'Thu 19 Oct 2023 - Fri 27 Oct 2023'
I want a formula in Q2 that will extract from P2 'Thu 19 Oct 2023' and dispaly it as 19 10 2023
I want a formula in R2 that will extract from Q2 '19 10 2023' and dispaly 19
I want a formula in S2 that will extract from Q2 '19 10 2023' and dispaly 10
I want a formula in T2 that will extract from Q2 '19 10 2023' and dispaly 2023
I want a formula in U2 that will extract from P2 'Fri 27 Oct 2023' and dispaly it as 27 10 2023
I want a formula in V2 that will extract from Q2 '27 10 2023' and dispaly 27
I want a formula in W2 that will extract from Q2 '27 10 2023' and dispaly 10
I want a formula in X2 that will extract from Q2 '27 10 2023' and display 2023

To extract specific parts of the date from cell Q2, you can use the LEFT, MID, and RIGHT functions. Here are the formulas you can use for each cell:

Q2: =SUBSTITUTE(LEFT(P2, FIND(" -", P2)-1), "Thu ", "")
R2: =LEFT(Q2, FIND(" ", Q2)-1)
S2: =MID(Q2, FIND(" ", Q2)+1, FIND(" ", Q2, FIND(" ", Q2)+1)-FIND(" ", Q2)-1)
T2: =RIGHT(Q2, LEN(Q2)-FIND(" ", Q2, FIND(" ", Q2)+1)-1)

U2: =SUBSTITUTE(RIGHT(P2, FIND(" -", P2)-1), "Fri ", "")
V2: =LEFT(U2, FIND(" ", U2)-1)
W2: =MID(U2, FIND(" ", U2)+1, FIND(" ", U2, FIND(" ", U2)+1)-FIND(" ", U2)-1)
X2: =RIGHT(U2, LEN(U2)-FIND(" ", U2, FIND(" ", U2)+1)-1)

These formulas take into account the specific format of the date in cell P2 and extract the desired parts accordingly.
The formula in Q2 displays '19 Oct 2023'. I want it to display '19 10 2023'. Even when I apply Custom Formatting to the cell to display as 'dd mm yyyy' it does not change it to '19 10 2023'

Apologies for the confusion. In that case, you can use the TEXT function to format the date in the desired format.

Use the following formula in Q2: 
=TEXT(DATEVALUE(MID(P2,5,11)),"dd mm yyyy")

This formula will extract the date from P2 (removing the 'Thu' at the beginning) using the MID function. The DATEVALUE function converts the extracted date from text to a date value, and then the TEXT function formats it as "dd mm yyyy".
The formula in T2 '=RIGHT(Q2, LEN(Q2)-FIND(" ", Q2, FIND(" ", Q2)+1)-1)' displays 023 and not 2023

Apologies for the oversight. To extract the year correctly, you can modify the formula in T2 as follows:

=T2

Since the year is already in the correct format, you can simply reference cell T2 to display it correctly.

However, if you want to make sure the formula extracts the year correctly, you can use the following formula:

T2: =MID(Q2, FIND(" ", Q2, FIND(" ", Q2, FIND(" ", Q2)+1)+1)+1, LEN(Q2))

This formula uses the MID function to extract the year from Q2. It searches for the third space character and starts extracting the text from the character after it. This will ensure that the formula correctly extracts the year portion from the date.

For the value in P2 which is Thu 19 Oct 2023 - Fri 27 Oct 2023,
you changed '=SUBSTITUTE(LEFT(P2, FIND(" -", P2)-1), "Thu ", "")' to '=TEXT(DATEVALUE(MID(P2,5,11)),"dd mm yyyy")' which provides the required output.

Can you please change 'U2: =SUBSTITUTE(RIGHT(P2, FIND(" -", P2)-1), "Fri ", "")'

Apologies for the oversight. Here is the corrected formula for U2:

U2: =TEXT(DATEVALUE(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2))), "dd mm yyyy")

This formula extracts the date after the hyphen "-" in cell P2 and uses the DATEVALUE function to convert it into a date value. Then, the TEXT function formats the date as "dd mm yyyy".
I get a ~VALUE error

I apologize for the error. It seems like there may be an issue with the format or structure of your input data. To better assist you, please provide the exact content of cell P2 (e.g., "Thu 19 Oct 2023 - Fri 27 Oct 2023") so that I can provide an accurate formula for the desired output.
The exact content of P2 is:  Thu 19 Oct 2023 - Fri 27 Oct 2023

Apologies for the confusion. Based on the provided information, you can use the following formulas to extract the required values:

U2: `=TRIM(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2)))`
V2: `=LEFT(Q2, FIND(" ", Q2)-1)`
W2: `=MID(Q2, FIND(" ", Q2)+1, FIND(" ", Q2, FIND(" ", Q2)+1)-FIND(" ", Q2)-1)`
X2: `=RIGHT(Q2, 4)`

Make sure that the cells in Q2 and P2 are properly formatted as dates so that the formulas work correctly.
Please correct this formula: =DATEVALUE(TRIM(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2))),"dd mm yyyy")

Apologies for the incorrect formula. To extract the date value from P2 and convert it into a proper date format, you can use the following formula for U2:

U2: `=DATEVALUE(TRIM(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2))))`

This formula extracts the date portion after the hyphen "-" in cell P2 using the MID function. The TRIM function is used to remove any extra spaces. Finally, the DATEVALUE function converts the extracted date into a proper date format.

The value dispayed in P2 is 'Thu 19 Oct 2023 - Fri 27 Oct 2023'.
When I use the formula '=TRIM(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2)))' in U2, it dispalys 'Fri 27 Oct 2023'
When I use the formula '=TEXT(DATEVALUE(MID(P2,5,11)),"dd mm yyyy")' in Q2 it dispalys '19 10 2023'
I want the formula in U2 to dispaly similar to what is diplayed in Q2.
I tried '=TEXT(DATEVALUE(TRIM(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2))),"dd mm yyyy"))' but I am getting an error 'you have entered too many arguments for this function'

Apologies for the confusion. To display the same date value in U2 as in Q2, you can use the following formula:

U2: `=TEXT(DATEVALUE(TRIM(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2)))), "dd mm yyyy")`

This formula first extracts the date portion after the hyphen "-" in cell P2 using the MID function. The TRIM function is used to remove any extra spaces. Then, the DATEVALUE function converts the extracted date into a proper date format. Finally, the TEXT function formats the date as "dd mm yyyy".
I am still getting a #VALUE! error

Apologies for the confusion. It seems that the error is occurring due to the date format in your system. Let's try a different approach that should work. Try using the following formula for U2:

U2: `=TEXT(DATEVALUE(RIGHT(P2,LEN(P2)-FIND("-",P2)-1)),"dd mm yyyy")`

This formula will extract the portion of the date after the hyphen "-" in cell P2 using the RIGHT and FIND functions. Then, the DATEVALUE function converts the extracted date into a proper date format, and the TEXT function formats it as "dd mm yyyy".

still getting the #VALUE! error for 'U2: =TEXT(DATEVALUE(RIGHT(P2,LEN(P2)-FIND("-",P2)-1)),"dd mm yyyy")'. The formula in in Q2 '=TEXT(DATEVALUE(MID(P2,5,11)),“dd mm yyyy”)' displays correctly. Is there a way to adopt this formula method to U2

Apologies for the continued issues. If the formula in Q2 is working correctly and providing the desired output, you can adopt a similar approach for U2. Here's the modified formula:

U2: `=TEXT(DATEVALUE(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2))), "dd mm yyyy")`

This formula extracts the date portion after the hyphen "-" in cell P2 using the MID function. The DATEVALUE function converts the extracted date into a proper date format, and the TEXT function formats it as "dd mm yyyy".

Please make sure to format the cells as text or apply a custom format to display the extracted dates correctly.
The formula '=TRIM(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2)))' displays the value as 'Fri 27 Oct 2023' but as a text value. How can I change this to a Date value

Apologies for the confusion. To convert the extracted text value to a date value in U2, you can use the DATE function along with the MID function and the DATEVALUE function. Here's the modified formula:

U2: `=DATEVALUE(MID(P2, FIND("-", P2)+2, LEN(P2)-FIND("-", P2)-2))`

This formula extracts the text value after the hyphen "-" in cell P2 using the MID function. The DATEVALUE function then converts the extracted text into a date value.

Make sure to format the cell as a date to display the extracted date correctly.

The value displayed in P2 is 'Thu 19 Oct 2023 - Fri 27 Oct 2023'.
How can I extract 'Thu 19 Oct 2023' to Q2

To extract 'Thu 19 Oct 2023' from cell P2 and display it in Q2, you can use the following formula:

Q2: `=LEFT(P2, FIND(" -", P2) - 1)`

This formula uses the LEFT function to extract the text from the beginning until the position of the hyphen "-" minus 1. It will extract 'Thu 19 Oct 2023' from cell P2 and display it in Q2.

The value displayed in P2 is ‘Thu 19 Oct 2023 - Fri 27 Oct 2023’.
How can I extract ‘Fri 27 Oct 2023’ to V2

To extract 'Fri 27 Oct 2023' from cell P2 and display it in V2, you can use the following formula:

V2: `=TRIM(RIGHT(P2,LEN(P2)-FIND("-",P2)-1))`

This formula uses the RIGHT function to extract the text after the hyphen "-" in cell P2. The TRIM function is then used to remove any leading or trailing spaces. It will extract 'Fri 27 Oct 2023' from cell P2 and display it in V2.
The text value in V2 is ‘Fri 27 Oct 2023’ , how will I convert this to a date value in W2

To convert the text value 'Fri 27 Oct 2023' to a date value in cell W2, you can use the DATEVALUE function together with the MID function to extract the date portion. Here's the formula:

W2: `=DATEVALUE(MID(V2, FIND(" ", V2)+1, LEN(V2)))`

This formula uses the MID function to extract the date portion from 'Fri 27 Oct 2023' by skipping the day of the week. The DATEVALUE function then converts the extracted text into a date value. The result will be displayed as a date value in cell W2.
I am getting a #VALUE error

Apologies for the confusion. If you are receiving a #VALUE error in W2 when trying to convert the text value in V2 to a date value, it is likely because the text format of 'Fri 27 Oct 2023' is not recognized as a valid date format by the DATEVALUE function.

To resolve this, you can use a combination of the DATE, MID, RIGHT, and LEFT functions. Here's the updated formula for W2:

W2: `=DATE(RIGHT(V2, 4), MID(V2, FIND(" ", V2)+1, 2), MID(V2, FIND(" ", V2)+4, 2))`

This formula extracts the year, month, and day from the text value in V2 using the RIGHT, MID, and LEFT functions. The DATE function then combines these values to form a proper date value in W2.

Remember to format the cell as a date to display the extracted date correctly.
I am still getting a value error

I apologize for the continued issue. It seems that there might be an inconsistency with the date format or the data in the cell. If you are unable to convert the text value in V2 to a date value, it is possible that the format of the text is not recognized as a valid date format.

To overcome this, you can try the following steps:

1. In a blank cell, enter the following formula: `=DATE(RIGHT(V2, 4), MID(V2, FIND(" ", V2, FIND(" ", V2)+1)+1, 2), MID(V2, FIND(" ", V2)+1, 2))`

2. Copy the result from the blank cell.

3. In cell W2, right-click and choose "Paste Values" to paste the copied result as a value.

This will paste the converted date value as a static value in cell W2. Please make sure to format the cell as a date to display the extracted date correctly.
still getting the same #VALUE! error

I apologize for the confusion. Based on the earlier conversation, it appears that the date format or other factors may be causing difficulties in extracting and converting the date value.

To better assist you, could you please provide the specific content of the cells P2 and V2? This will help me understand the format and structure of the data and provide a more accurate solution.

The value in P2 is derived from a formula =LIST!O2 and the value dispalyed is Thu 19 Oct 2023 - Fri 27 Oct 2023.
The value in V2 is derived from a formula =RIGHT(P2, FIND("_ ",P2) - 1 and the value displayed is Fri 27 Oct 2023

Apologies for the confusion. Based on the provided information, here are the formulas to extract the desired values:

In Q2, use the formula `=LEFT(P2, FIND(" ", P2)-1)`. This will extract 'Thu 19 Oct 2023' from cell P2 and display it in Q2.

In W2, use the formula `=LEFT(V2, FIND(" ", V2)-1)`. This will extract 'Fri' from cell V2 and display it in W2.

In X2, use the formula `=MID(V2, FIND(" ", V2)+1, FIND(" ", V2, FIND(" ", V2)+1)-FIND(" ", V2)-1)`. This will extract '27' from cell V2 and display it in X2.

In Y2, use the formula `=RIGHT(V2, 4)`. This will extract '2023' from cell V2 and display it in Y2.

Make sure to format the cells as needed to display the extracted values correctly.

The value in P2 is derived from a formula =LIST!O2 and the value dispalyed is Thu 19 Oct 2023 - Fri 27 Oct 2023.
The value in V2 is derived from a formula =RIGHT(P2, FIND("_ ",P2) - 1 and the value dispalyed is Fri 27 Oct 2023.
I want the value of W2 to be displayed in date format dd mm yy

To convert the text value in W2 ('Fri') to a date format in cell X2, you can use the DATEVALUE function. Here's the modified formula for W2:

W2: `=DATEVALUE(V2)`

This formula uses the DATEVALUE function to convert the text value in V2 ('Fri 27 Oct 2023') to a date value.

To display the result in the desired date format 'dd mm yy' in cell X2, you can use the TEXT function. Here's the formula for X2:

X2: `=TEXT(W2, "dd mm yy")`

This formula uses the TEXT function to format the date value from W2 in the desired format 'dd mm yy'.
